title: RandomAccessFile vs. Sequential Access
description: A visual comparison of how data is accessed sequentially versus randomly using the RandomAccessFile class, highlighting the role of the file pointer.
elements: Data File (as a block of bytes with indices)
relationships: Sequential streams read/write bytes one after another, moving forward only
RandomAccessFile uses a file pointer to jump to any position (byte offset) in the file using seek()
read() and write() operations occur at the current position of the file pointer, which then advances
